note: HARNESS FLOOR probe: A with an identity transform should render == imageA in BOTH engines. FINDING 2026-07-15: headless matches A (mae 1.09) but TS diverges (mae 16.5), worst at the TOP rows (mae~140) — the TS drop-zone CONFORM/fit differs from FCP on the bare skeleton (centre pixel is identical, so it is edge/framing, not colour). The synthetic scene needs the drop-zone Fit/Type/dim params that real slugs carry before per-primitive deltas are trustworthy. This is the FIRST capability to close.

Layer Position X +300px (Motion centre origin, +x=right). WORKING: both engines apply it; ~19dB residual = a sub-pixel/edge-sampling divergence in the compositor blit, the first concrete transform capability to close.
Layer Position Y +200px. WORKING (hvi 71): verifies the y-axis sign (Motion +y=UP). ~18dB residual, same blit-sampling gap as position.x.
